_source: r/aoe2 community charts: build-order flowchart (post ijgxll) + unique-unit response/notes table (preview.redd.it fcplfb2y5w8h1) + revised unit response chart (post 1kvz7mo)

build_orders.intro: Five beginner build orders sharing one Dark Age opening, then diverging. Villager counts at Feudal: FC Unique Unit 28+2, FC Boom 27+2, FC Knights 28+2, Scouts 22 (transitions to Archers/Skirms/Castle), Archers 23. 'FC' = the three Fast Castle builds (Unique Unit, Boom, Knights).
shared_opening: 2) 4 villagers on wood   [Scouts build: 3 to Scouts]
3) 1 villager lures boar [Scouts build: 2 to Scouts]
5) 2 lure boar / 2 farms [Fast Castle builds: go Fast Castle here; Archers build: 2 boar / 1 farm to Archers]
6) 5 villagers on wood   [Scouts build: 4 Scouts] [Knights build: 6 to FC Knights]

builds.name: FC Unique Unit
path: Fast Castle (+3 gold, 2 on Unique Unit)
Fast Castle: +8 farms, 7 on Unique Unit

builds.name: Scouts
path: Transition to Archers: 2 Ranges, +10 gold, +18 farms
Transition to Skirms: Range, +2 wood, +4 gold, +18 farms

shared_milestones: All 5 builds: build a Blacksmith in Feudal
Common buildings by build appear at the Barracks / Stable / Range / Market / 2nd TC stage

Armenians.notes: Can heal units and pick up relics.

Aztecs.notes: Gains +1 attack for each unit killed (up to 4).

Bengalis.notes: Can switch between ranged and melee attack mode.

Berbers.notes: Can passively regenerate HP with unique technology.
Trained in archery ranges. Has a minimum range to attack. All teammates can train them in team games.

Bohemians.notes: Fires 5 additional bullets. Halves damage from enemy projectiles passing through its hitbox by taking 1 damage.

Bohemians.bonus: Buildings ++++, Ships +++, Stone defense +++, Heavy siege weapons +++, Siege weapons ++
Bohemians.notes: Trained in siege workshops. Upgrades the Bombard Cannon.

Britons.notes: Archer with the longest range when fully upgraded.

Bulgarians.notes: Turns into an infantry unit upon death (dismounted Konnik).

Burgundians.notes: Charges its attack over 40 seconds, dealing +20 (+25 elite) on the first enemy hit.

Burgundians.bonus: Cavalry +++, Camels +++, Ships +++, Elephants +++, Shock infantry +
Burgundians.notes: HP and attack upgrade automatically when aging up (Castle and Imperial). All villagers can be turned into Flemish Militia at once with unique tech.

Byzantines.notes: High cavalry armor (stronger against spearmen than regular cavalry).

Chinese.notes: Fires 3 arrows in succession (5 for elite). First arrow deals full damage, additional arrows deal 3 pierce damage.

Cumans.notes: Fires a burst of 3 arrows (4 for elite). Only 1 deals full damage, additional arrows deal 3 pierce damage.

Dravidians.notes: Charges +12 (+15 elite) over 24 (20 elite) seconds, then a blast attack of 50% strength in a small radius.

Ethiopians.notes: Trains fast and has good movement speed.

Franks.notes: Deals melee damage instead of pierce.

Georgians.notes: Receives +1 (up to +4) attack for every 7 Monaspa or knights close by.

Goths.notes: High pierce armor. Can be trained in Barracks and very fast with unique techs.

Gurjaras.notes: 100% pass-through damage directly behind. Deals melee damage (ranged infantry).
Trained in stables. Can dodge 5 (7 elite) consecutive projectiles in a 20-second window.
Upgrades to the regular camel lines automatically in Castle Age.

Hindustanis.notes: Attacks pass through the target to units behind it (50% damage). Good pierce armor.
Upgrades the Heavy Camel Rider from the regular camel line.

Incas.notes: Has a longer range than regular infantry.

Italians.notes: Stronger against cavalry than regular archers.
Trained in Barracks. Teammates can train them in team games. Negates some damage from gunpowder unit attacks.

Japanese.notes: Gains +1 attack and 25% speed when charging a nearby enemy.

Jurchens.notes: Ignores one melee attack every 40 (30) seconds. Takes -50% friendly fire.
Trained in archery ranges. Throws explosives with area damage. Has minimum range. Friendly fire deals -50%.

Khitans.notes: Deals 9 additional bleeding damage to non-siege units over 3 seconds.
Trained in siege workshops. Projectiles spawn an area hazard.

Lithuanians.notes: Attacks ignore the armor of non-siege land units.

Magyars.notes: Gold cost can be replaced with food via unique technology.

Malians.notes: Deals melee damage instead of pierce.

Mapuche.notes: Deals increased damage to injured targets.
Fires a shot every 30s with +2 (+3 elite) attack that slows targets 15% for 10s. Can gain pass-through damage from unique tech.

Maya.notes: High HP and movement speed compared to regular archers.

Mongols.notes: Fires quicker than regular cavalry archers.

Muisca.notes: Trained from Barracks and Monasteries. Attacks faster the longer it stays in combat.

Persians.notes: Deals 50% damage to nearby units. Harder to convert than regular units.

Poles.notes: Reduces target armor and pierce armor by 1 with every attack.

Portuguese.notes: Fires 5 projectiles in a cone, each dealing full damage.

Romans.notes: Increases movement and attack speed of nearby militia-line units.
Trained in Barracks. Replaces the Two-Handed Swordsman and Champion (militia line).

Saracens.notes: Deals melee damage instead of pierce.

Shu.notes: Snares non-siege enemies, slowing them 15% for 10s (does not stack). Gains HP near each other via unique tech.
Trained in siege workshops. Replaces the scorpion line. Swaps modes - Barrage: many projectiles hitting 1 target each; Focus fire: fewer projectiles with pass-through.

Spanish.notes: Mounted gunpowder unit; mobile and strong vs buildings.
Monk with fast movement speed. Can heal, convert and pick up relics.

Tatars.bonus: Buildings ++++, Elephants ++++, Cavalry ++, Camels +++, Siege weapons ++
Tatars.notes: Trained from siege workshops. Suicides upon death (huge bonus vs elephants/cavalry).

Teutons.notes: High melee armor. Slow movement speed.

Tupi.notes: Costs 0.5 population space. Trained in pairs. Can deal poison damage via unique tech.
Trained from Barracks. Deals full damage to the target and the unit right behind it.

Turks.notes: Stronger version of a Hand Cannoneer.

Vietnamese.notes: High pierce armor compared to regular archers.
Trained from archery ranges. Upgrades the Elite Skirmisher line. Teammates can train them in team games.

Wei.notes: Gains attack and HP as it kills enemy units, up to 4.
Trained from archery ranges. Replaces the cavalry archer line. Fires a 5-arrow volley every 30s.

Wu.notes: Vs units: shorter range, multiple arrows with area damage. Vs buildings: longer range, single arrow.
Trained from Barracks. Gains speed and attack but loses armor below 45 HP.
